pages.full_text:
S, E, J & screaming C.  He, of course, won, pushing them in.  A leisurely afternoon swimming + boating + reading.  A perfect site
__July 22nd__
	Today we actually made it to Conques looked round the shops + church + had lunch in a friendly restaurant.  A woman was playing the piano in the church — rehearsing for a recital that evening.  It was beautiful.  The tympanum is awe-inspiring + very well preserved  it was too hot to walk down to see the old pilgrim's bridge, but we did look at the treasures + find out about St. Foy.
	After we arrived back at camp, the boys had a pedalo fight with the Dutch, + in the evening a treasure hunt was organised
__July 23rd__
	Rain poured early on, then the day warmed up.  John took himself down to the pool to swim — fortunately with
pages.notes: Page begins mid-sentence continuing from previous spread — 'S, E, J & screaming C' refers to people (probably children/family members by initial). The handwritten page number at top-left is partially cut off; reads as '94' based on sequence from nearby transcripts (pdf_page 45 = diary pages 86-87, so pdf_page 49 ~ pages 94-95). Conques is a village in Aveyron, France, known for its Romanesque abbey church with a tympanum and the relics of Ste Foy. 'St. Foy' refers to Sainte-Foy, whose relics are housed at Conques. Year not stated on page; trip is in France in late July, consistent with early volume position (1989 or early 1990s) — iso_guess year tentative. 'pedalo' = pedal boat.

pages.full_text:
armbands.
	In the evening we went to Hotel du Nord at Montealey.  It was described in the book as "cosy".  Indeed, it was, + relaxing + good food.  Stuffed cabbage, veal + lovely puddings.  Myrtle pie / chestnut + pistachio icecream.  We also had a local dish — mashed potato, stretchy cheese + garlic.

__July 24th__
	It's cool + rainy today.  We set off early on a day trip to see Estaing and Espalion .  Lovely old houses, chateaux + churches.  In the evening the children played ping pong + other ball games with other children at the camp.  The weather warmed up in the evening.

__July 25th__
	We woke up at 6.45. + left at 7.45 for the canoe base on the river Lot.
pages.notes: Page continues from left page mid-sentence ('armbands'). Handwritten page number '95' visible at top-right. 'Montealey' is the diary's spelling — likely Montesalvy (Cantal, France), a village near Conques; could also read 'Montsalvy'. 'Hotel du Nord at Montealey' — Hotel du Nord is the restaurant/hotel name. Estaing is a village on the river Lot in Aveyron; Espalion is a nearby town also on the Lot. 'stretchy cheese' is likely aligot, a traditional dish of the Aubrac region. Year not on page; same trip as left page — iso_guess year tentative. The canoe base on the 'river Lot' at the end of July 25th entry is consistent with the Lot valley near Estaing/Espalion.
